title: TB12 Shake
cScaleSummary: C0 because the recipe contains no animal-derived ingredients; almond milk, peanut butter, flax seeds, chia seeds, cacao nibs, spinach, banana, blueberries, and the remaining ingredients contribute 0 mg cholesterol.
ldlActiveSummary: LDL-Active because the recipe is built around fiber-forward seeds, fruit, spinach, and unsaturated fats, contains no saturated animal fat, and delivers structure and satiety through whole-food ingredients rather than cream or animal fat.
intro: This shake sits at the intersection of performance nutrition and real breakfast. Built with cacao, peanut butter, and fruit, it drinks like a chocolate-peanut milkshake but behaves like a disciplined morning meal. Seeds provide fiber and texture, fruit gives natural sweetness, and high-power blending turns everything into a silky emulsion. The result is satisfying, clean, and built for consistency.

stepGroups.title: Instructions
items: Add almond milk and protein powder to the blender carafe and pulse a few seconds to mix. Llet this sit for 1 or 2 minutes to hydrate.

Add the water, peanut butter, flax seeds, chia seeds, cacao nibs, and sea salt to the carafe. Start on low speed 1 and ramp to high speed. Blend about 30 to 40 seconds until the seeds and nibs are fully broken down and the mixture looks creamy.
Add spinach, frozen banana, and frozen blueberries. Blend on high about 30 seconds until completely smooth. The spinach should disappear into the mixture.
Add the ice and blend on high until the sound of the blender smooths out and the shake becomes thick and silky, about 20 to 30 seconds.
Serve immediately. Each portion should be thick, cold, and milkshake-smooth.

stepGroups.title: FYI
items: This makes a very appetizing looking, military green colored shake. Tastes great though!
